prompt:
Three internal services now talk to the same RPC gateway and each wrote its own JSON-RPC framing. One garbles batch responses because it assumes responses come back in the same order as the requests (the gateway is async, they don't). One silently drops errors from batch items. One forgets to strip the id field from notifications and gets parse errors back. I want one shared jsonrpc.ts, no npm packages, that all of them can import. test_jsonrpc.ts is the acceptance contract: `node --test test_jsonrpc.ts` fully green means done; test file read-only. We run node 26 with type stripping — no enums, no namespaces, no parameter properties, explicit .ts import extensions.

Note: go-telewire is a pipe-delimited ENCODING performance seed with no overlap. This seed is about JSON-RPC 2.0 PROTOCOL SEMANTICS: id correlation, batch order-independence, notification framing, and typed errors.

Transport type: (req: string) => Promise<string>. The transport receives the full serialised JSON-RPC request (or batch array) and returns the full serialised response (or '' for notifications, which the client ignores).

export class JsonRpcClient
  constructor(transport: (req: string) => Promise<string>)

  call(method: string, params: object | unknown[] | null): Promise<unknown>
    Build {jsonrpc:'2.0', id:<auto>, method, params} (omit params key if null).
    Await the transport, parse the JSON-RPC response, and:
    - success: return result
    - error: throw JsonRpcError
    Id generation: any scheme that produces unique ids across concurrent calls (integer counter is fine).

  notify(method: string, params: object | unknown[] | null): Promise<void>
    Build {jsonrpc:'2.0', method, params} — NO id field. Send via transport, ignore the return value entirely.

  batch(requests: Array<{method: string, params: object | unknown[] | null}>): Promise<Array<unknown | JsonRpcError>>
    Build a JSON array of JSON-RPC 2.0 request objects, each with a unique id.
    Await the transport, parse the response array.
    Match each response to its request by id — order in the response array may differ from the request order.
    Return results in REQUEST order (position 0 = first request, etc.).
    A success entry → its result value; an error entry → a JsonRpcError instance (not thrown).
    Stray ids (ids not in this batch) are silently ignored. Duplicate ids (same id twice in response) keep the first.

export class JsonRpcError extends Error
  code: number
  message: string  (from the error object, also set on Error.message)
  data: unknown    (undefined if absent)
  constructor(code: number, message: string, data?: unknown)
